blockingIssues.description: The new regression coverage only exercises `warcraft_status`; the feature's expected behavior also requires doctor/status parity for blocked tasks, but this feature neither changes doctor behavior nor adds a doctor-tool assertion proving blocked tasks remain coherently represented there. Because the targeted seam command can skip entirely, the implemented evidence does not fully cover the contract that blocked status and doctor surfaces remain non-contradictory.

The attribution repair record claims `worktree-create-smoke-timeout-fix` is attributable to commit `0c72ab7`, but `git show --stat 0c72ab7` shows a broad lane-based E2E reorganization touching 14 files, not a feature-scoped smoke-timeout fix commit. This does not meet the feature requirement to align history with the actual implementing commit(s) or an explicitly supported verification-only trail for the smoke-timeout feature.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify validator reporting when a command exits 0 but skips all assertions, so workers and reviewers record it as skip-only evidence rather than treating it as a full validation pass.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature scrutiny-blocked-status-contract-fix observed host-preflight seam tests exiting 0 while skipping assertions; evidence: handoff 2026-03-11T08-22-20-598Z__scrutiny-blocked-status-contract-fix__fd8a2d59-79af-4334-9892-b27452c03f2c.json notes `bun test .../blocked-resume-seam.test.ts` exited 0 with both host-lane tests skipped; .factory/skills/audit-fix-worker/SKILL.md step 6/9 does not mention recording skip-only validators separately.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Define acceptable evidence for verification-only attribution repair, requiring referenced behavior commits or records to be feature-scoped or explicitly justified as the actual implementing artifact.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature scrutiny-feature-attribution-repair cited a broad unrelated commit during verification-only attribution repair; evidence: .factory/skills/audit-fix-worker/SKILL.md step 12 says to create a minimal feature-scoped attribution/verification commit or return for a supported verification-only decision, but it does not require that referenced behavior commits be scoped to the feature under repair.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add mission guidance that required `.factory/library/` validation artifacts may need explicit `git add -f` when the repo gitignore excludes `.factory/`, so workers do not miss required mission records.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature scrutiny-feature-attribution-repair reported `.factory/library/` attribution records needed forced staging because `.factory/` is ignored; evidence: Worker handoff for `scrutiny-feature-attribution-repair` records that `.factory/library/scrutiny-feature-attribution-repair.json` needed `git add -f`; repo service manifest at `.factory/services.yaml` does not mention this caveat.
